title: 10 best herbal capsules for premenstrual syndrome
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for premenstrual syndrome are natural supplements designed to alleviate symptoms associated with PMS, such as bloating, mood swings, and cramps. These capsules contain a blend of herbs like chasteberry, ginger, and St. John's Wort that help regulate hormonal imbalances, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. For instance, chasteberry has been shown to decrease symptoms of breast tenderness, anxiety, and irritability, while ginger helps alleviate physical discomforts like cramps and bloating. By using these herbal capsules, women can significantly improve their quality of life, feeling more confident and in control during the premenstrual phase.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain phytoestrogens, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to PMS symptoms. The capsules work by stimulating the pituitary gland to produce prolactin, a hormone that helps to balance estrogen levels in the body. As a result, chaste tree capsules may alleviate symptoms such as breast tenderness, mood swings, and bloating associated with premenstrual syndrome. They also help to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce anxiety and fatigue.
remedy_properties: Agonist action on dopamine receptors: Chaste tree capsules contain flavonoids and iridoid glycosides that mimic the effects of dopamine, helping to regulate hormonal imbalances associated with PMS, such as irregular cycles and mood swings.

Progestogenic activity: The herb's progestogenic compounds help to normalize progesterone levels, which can become unbalanced during the luteal phase of the menstrual cycle, leading to symptoms like breast tenderness and bloating.

Antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects: Chaste tree capsules contain compounds that relax smooth muscle tissue and reduce inflammation, helping to alleviate PMS symptoms like cramps, back pain, and mood swings.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chaste tree flowers contain a high concentration of flavonoids and iridoid glycosides, which help regulate hormones and alleviate PMS symptoms such as bloating and breast tenderness.
Seeds: The seeds of the chaste tree plant are rich in essential oils, including beta-caryophyllene, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce PMS-related cramps and pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried vitex agnus-castus fruit and high-quality gelatin capsules in 500 mg size.
Grind 1 part dried vitex agnus-castus fruit into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 250 mg of cornstarch to improve flowability through the capsules.
Fill 500 mg gelatin capsules with approximately 250-300 mg of the mixed powder preparation taking about 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain rosmarinic acid, a compound that has been shown to effectively alleviate symptoms of PMS. By calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation, lemon balm capsules can reduce anxiety and stress associated with PMS. Additionally, their anti-inflammatory properties can help ease cramps and bloating, while also regulating hormonal imbalances that contribute to mood swings and breast tenderness. Overall, herbal lemon balm capsules offer a natural and gentle approach to managing PMS symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Lemon balm capsules have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress symptoms associated with PMS.
Hormone-balancing properties: The herb's ability to regulate estrogen and progesterone levels can help reduce PMS symptoms such as mood swings, bloating, and breast tenderness.

Anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties: Lemon balm capsules contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help soothe cramps, headaches, and other physical discomforts associated with PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant are used to create medicinal capsules for premenstrual syndrome because they contain a high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.

Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant are used to make medicinal capsules for premenstrual syndrome due to their high content of flavonoids, which help to reduce anxiety and stress associated with PMS symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested melissa officinalis leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 12 hours or until crumbly.
Grind the dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix one part melissa officinalis powder with three parts organic cornstarch and fill empty capsules according to dosage label instructions.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms associated with PMS. The capsules work by regulating hormonal imbalances, reducing water retention and bloating, and soothing emotional distress. By promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system, Chinese peony capsules can help to reduce irritability, anxiety, and depression commonly experienced during the premenstrual phase. Additionally, they can also help to regulate menstrual cramps, heavy bleeding, and breast tenderness, making them a natural and effective solution for managing PMS symptoms.
remedy_properties: Damping heat and resolving inflammation: This property helps to reduce symptoms such as breast tenderness, bloating, and mood swings by calming down heat and inflammation in the body.
Regulating blood flow and menstrual cycle: Chinese Peony's ability to regulate blood flow and menstrual cycle helps to alleviate cramps, heavy bleeding, and other hormonal-related PMS symptoms.
Soothing anxiety and stress: The herb's calming effects on the nervous system help to reduce feelings of anxiety, irritability, and emotional instability that are common in PMS sufferers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help alleviate PMS symptoms such as breast tenderness and mood swings.
Rhyzomes: Containing saponins and flavonoids, rhyzomes exhibit estrogen-regulating properties, which can help mitigate PMS symptoms like bloating and cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality paeonia lactiflora roots and dry them properly.
Grind the dried paeonia lactiflora roots into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar.
Mix 1000mg of the powdered root with 500mg of excipients such as silica and calcium carbonate.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal mixture and seal tightly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain natural ingredients that promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of bloating and breast tenderness. The herbal extract in valerian capsules has a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm the mind and body, and alleviating mood swings and irritability associated with PMS. Additionally, valerian root has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce water retention and alleviate cramps, making it an effective natural remedy for managing premenstrual symptoms.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian root helps to calm nervousness, anxiety, and restlessness associated with PMS, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels.
Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian root's muscle-relaxing effects can help alleviate cramps, backaches, and other physical symptoms often experienced during PMS.
Antioxidant properties: Valerian root's antioxidant activity helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which may contribute to PMS symptoms such as breast tenderness and mood changes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has been shown to have anxiolytic and sedative effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of PMS.
Rhizomes: Rich in essential oils and alkaloids, these underground stems are believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce cramps and discomfort associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valeriana officinalis roots and stems in 50-gram quantities.
Grind the dried valeriana officinalis into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 250 milligrams of ground valeriana officinalis with 500 milligrams of gelatin and 100 milligrams of tapioca starch in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and seal tightly using a capsule machine or manual filling method for 15 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months and use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because it has a calming effect on the body, soothing symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, and mood swings. The herb's natural anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce breast tenderness and bloating that can occur during PMS. Additionally, chamomile's sedative effects promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night. By addressing these common PMS symptoms, chamomile capsules offer a natural and effective way to manage discomfort and improve overall well-being before and during menstruation.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: Chamomile's ability to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation helps alleviate symptoms such as irritability, mood swings, and restlessness associated with PMS.

Anti-Inflammatory and Antispasmodic Properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness, while its antispasmodic effects can ease muscle and uterine spasms that may contribute to PMS symptoms.

Hormone-Regulating Effects: Chamomile has been shown to have a mild estrogenic effect, which may help regulate hormonal imbalances associated with PMS, reducing symptoms such as mood swings, breast tenderness, and hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe menstrual cramps and calms nervous tension associated with PMS.
Roots: Contain camazol, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of bloating and breast tenderness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers weighing 100g for optimal pms relief.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 5g of the ground chamomile powder with 95g of filler material such as cornstarch or potato starch.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the chamomile mixture using an automatic capsule filler within 30 seconds.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years or 1 year beyond the expiration date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that alleviate symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings. The capsules' natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce water retention, easing discomfort and pain associated with PMS. Additionally, angelica's essential oils have a calming effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, helping women feel more balanced and prepared for their periods.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Angelica's antispasmodic properties help relieve cramps and spasms in the uterus, reducing pain and discomfort associated with PMS.
Estrogen-regulating properties: Angelica's estrogen-regulating effects may help balance hormonal fluctuations that contribute to symptoms of PMS, such as mood swings, breast tenderness, and bloating.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Angelica's anti-inflammatory properties may reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms like breast pain, swelling, and joint pain associated with PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the angelica plant are used to make medicinal capsules for premenstrual syndrome because they contain high amounts of coumarins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as cramps and mood swings.

Rhizomes: Rhizomes of the angelica plant are used to make medicinal capsules for premenstrual syndrome because they contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which have been shown to have calming and sedative effects that can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried angelica archangelica root and stems from a trusted source.
Cleanse the herbs by sieving through a fine-mesh screen to remove debris and impurities.
Grind the cleaned herbs into a fine powder using an herb grinder or coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered herb with 200ml of glycerin and 400ml of water in a saucepan, heating it to 60°c for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture and seal immediately, storing them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain a blend of herbs that target the root causes of PMS symptoms. The capsules contain Dong Quai, which is known for its ability to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness. Additionally, the capsules may help to balance hormones, reducing mood swings and anxiety. By promoting a smooth flow of energy and circulation, Chinese date capsules can also help to reduce water retention and alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
remedy_properties: Relaxation-promoting flavonoids: The capsules contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which help to relax smooth muscles and alleviate symptoms like cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: The herbs in the capsules, including dates, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with PMS symptoms like mood swings, fatigue, and joint pain.

Serotonin-regulating alkaloids: Certain alkaloids present in the herbs, such as those found in licorice root (Glycyrrhizae), can help regulate serotonin levels, which may alleviate symptoms of anxiety, depression, and insomnia commonly experienced during PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, the roots help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome such as bloating and breast tenderness.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high amount of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes can help reduce stress and anxiety associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality dried ziziphus jujuba fruit and sift it through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any debris or impurities.
Grind the sifted ziziphus jujuba into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5-7 minutes until it reaches a uniform consistency.
Mix 400mg of the ground ziziphus jujuba with 100mg of silica gel and 50mg of magnesium stearate in a bowl for 30 seconds until well combined.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 500mg of the ziziphus jujuba blend and seal them immediately to preserve potency.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 1 year, preparing individual doses as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that have a calming effect on the body. These compounds help to alleviate symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Additionally, the capsules' antioxidant properties help to mitigate hormonal imbalances, further alleviating PMS symptoms and promoting overall well-being during this critical time of month.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Herbal Maidenhair Tree capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to PMS symptoms such as breast tenderness, bloating, and mood swings.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules' anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate cramps, headaches, and other physical symptoms associated with PMS by reducing prostaglandins and other inflammatory mediators.

Adaptogenic effects: Maidenhair Tree extracts have been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and adapt to stress, which can help regulate hormones and reduce anxiety, irritability, and other emotional symptoms commonly experienced during PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain bioactive compounds that help alleviate symptoms of PMS, such as cramps and bloating.
Barks: The barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in early morning after dew has evaporated, ensuring optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade drying or low-temperature dehydration to preserve bioactive compounds.
Grind 200mg of dried ginkgo biloba leaf into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking approximately 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 50mg of pure gelatin capsule filler in a small bowl, allowing for easy encapsulation.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the ginkgo biloba-gelatin mixture, then seal and label the capsules, requiring about 5 minutes per 100 capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its natural ability to ease symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and mood swings. The herb's active compounds, including anethole and fenchone, work together to relax the muscles, reduce inflammation, and regulate hormone production. By doing so, fennel capsules can help alleviate feelings of irritability, anxiety, and depression often associated with PMS. Additionally, its carminative properties help to release gas and alleviate bloating, providing a sense of relief and comfort during this time.
remedy_properties: Estrogenic properties: Fennel's estrogen-like compounds may help regulate hormonal imbalances, which are a common cause of PMS symptoms such as breast tenderness and mood swings.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Fennel's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce pelvic pain, cramping, and bloating associated with PMS by reducing inflammation in the uterus and digestive tract.
Antispasmodic actions: Fennel's ability to relax smooth muscles can alleviate cramps, spasms, and digestive discomfort often experienced during PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in estrogenic compounds, which can help alleviate symptoms of PMS such as bloating and breast tenderness.
Seeds: Containing flavonoids and anethole, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce severity of PMS symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare seeds in late summer when ripe and dry thoroughly for at least two weeks.
Grind 250 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 500 grams of gelatin capsules and fill each capsule to 750 milligrams per serving.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or in the refrigerator for longer storage.
Take two capsules three times daily starting from day one of menstruation and continuing until symptoms subside, ideally within 7-10 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain a natural blend of herbs that help alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and mood swings. The botanicals in these capsules work to balance hormones and reduce inflammation, providing relief from the physical and emotional discomfort associated with PMS. Additionally, beach rose has been shown to regulate menstrual cycles and improve overall hormonal health, making it a natural and effective solution for women seeking relief from premenstrual symptoms.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Beach Rose capsules contain flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, potentially helping to ease PMS symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in Beach Rose capsules may help regulate hormonal balance and reduce the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause cramping, pain, and mood disturbances associated with PMS.

Saponins: Saponins in Beach Rose capsules have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, irritability, and anxiety often experienced during the premenstrual phase.
remedy_parts: Roots: Beach rose roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in relieving cramps and bloating associated with premenstrual syndrome.

Flowers: The flowers of the beach rose plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce symptoms such as breast tenderness and mood swings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa x rugosa flowers at dawn on a full moon day for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator set at 150°f for 6 hours.
Crush 30g of dried rosa x rugosa flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 50mg of vitamin b6 and 20mg of st. john's wort extract in a glass jar for 1 hour.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they promote a sense of calmness and relaxation, alleviating symptoms such as anxiety, irritability, and mood swings. The calming properties of lavender also help to regulate hormonal fluctuations, reducing symptoms like bloating, breast tenderness, and cramps. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of lavender may help to soothe and calm the digestive system, which can be affected by PMS. By using English lavender capsules, women may find relief from the uncomfortable and debilitating symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and Sedative Effects: Lavender's calming properties help reduce anxiety, stress, and irritability, common PMS symptoms, by promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate physical symptoms like breast tenderness, bloating, and cramping associated with PMS by reducing inflammation and swelling.
Hormone Regulation: Lavender's estrogen-like properties can help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to PMS symptoms, such as mood swings, hot flashes, and mood changes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome, such as anxiety and mood swings.

Flowers: The flowers are a rich source of essential oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help relieve cramps and other physical symptoms associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried lavender flowers of lavandula angustifolia at the peak of potency and store them in an airtight container.
Grind 10 grams of dried lavender flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of ground lavender with 1000 milligrams of carrier material such as potato starch or silica gel in a bowl.
Fill empty #0 capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filling machine or spoon and compress gently.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red osier capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain extracts from the stems of the red-osier dogwood plant, which have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of PMS. The capsules work by regulating hormonal imbalances and calming the nervous system, reducing anxiety and mood swings associated with PMS. Additionally, red osier has anti-inflammatory properties that help ease physical symptoms such as breast tenderness and bloating. As a result, women who take red osier capsules often report improved overall well-being during their menstrual cycles.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory properties: Red Osier capsules can help relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which may reduce symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings associated with PMS.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbs in Red Osier capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help alleviate symptoms like cramping, pain, and irritability commonly experienced during the premenstrual phase.

Hormone-balancing properties: Some herbal extracts in Red Osier capsules, such as Vitex agnus-castus (Chasteberry), are believed to help regulate hormonal imbalances, particularly estrogen dominance, which can contribute to PMS symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate breast tenderness and mood swings associated with PMS.
Rhizomes: Rich in antioxidants, rhizomes help regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce symptoms of bloating and cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried cornus sericea leaves and stems in quantities of 100 grams.
Grind the herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 250 milliliters of boiling water for 20 minutes to create an infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container within 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the herbal powder and store in an airtight container for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain a natural diuretic that can help alleviate bloating, water retention, and breast tenderness. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in dandelion may also reduce cramps, mood swings, and fatigue associated with PMS. Additionally, dandelion's ability to regulate blood sugar levels can help manage cravings and stabilize energy levels. As a result, women taking dandelion capsules may experience reduced symptoms of PMS and improved overall well-being during this time.
remedy_properties: Diuretic Properties: Dandelion's natural diuretic properties help to reduce water retention and alleviate bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings commonly associated with PMS.
Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to symptoms such as cramps, headaches, and fatigue during PMS.

Antioxidant Activity: Dandelion's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and mood imbalance, potentially reducing symptoms of anxiety, depression, and irritability associated with PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in phytoestrogens, which help alleviate menstrual cramps and mood swings associated with PMS.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce breast tenderness and swelling caused by PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanly wash the roots with lukewarm water and gently scrub off dirt.
Dry the roots in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or use desiccant.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle or grinder.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 200mg of powdered dandelion root.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This natural ingredient can help alleviate symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings associated with PMS. Additionally, the flavonoids present in licorice root may also help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which are often linked to premenstrual syndrome. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal licorice capsules can provide relief from the physical and emotional discomforts of PMS.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Reduces inflammation and anxiety symptoms associated with PMS by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds and promoting relaxation.
Flavonoids: Acts as a natural antioxidant, protecting against oxidative stress and hormone imbalances that contribute to PMS symptoms such as breast tenderness and mood swings.
Isomaltose: Soothes digestive issues common during PMS, including bloating, cramps, and constipation by reducing inflammation and increasing the absorption of nutrients.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in relieving cramps, bloating, and mood swings associated with PMS.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to reduce cortisol levels and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression often experienced during PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Measure out 50g of the ground glycyrrhiza glabra powder and mix it with 250ml of boiling distilled water to create an herbal extract.
Allow the mixture to steep for 30 minutes in a heat-proof container, then strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Transfer the strained herbal extract to a dark glass bottle and let it cool to room temperature, then add 500ml of glycerin and 250ml of distilled water to create a tincture solution.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 400mg of the tincture solution using an encapsulation machine, then store them in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that help alleviate its symptoms. The capsules' bitter orange extract, also known as Citrus aurantium, has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety and irritability, which are common complaints during the premenstrual phase. Additionally, the capsules may help regulate hormonal imbalances, bloating, and mood swings associated with PMS, providing relief for women who struggle with these symptoms before their periods arrive.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Bitter orange capsules contain flavonoids that help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which can alleviate PMS symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings by reducing inflammation and water retention.

Adaptogenic effects: The capsule's herbal blend may help the body adapt to hormonal changes during the menstrual cycle, reducing stress and anxiety associated with PMS by promoting relaxation and balance in the body's physiological responses.

Antioxidant activity: Bitter orange capsules contain antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative damage caused by inflammation, which can contribute to PMS symptoms such as cramps, mood swings, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of bitter orange contain flavonoids and limonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing symptoms associated with PMS.
Seeds: Bitter orange seeds are a rich source of vitamin C and flavonoids, which can help alleviate symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest citrus x aurantium peel from fresh oranges and dry it thoroughly for at least two weeks.
Combine one part dried citrus x aurantium peel with one part dried green tea leaves in a mixing bowl.
Add 50 grams of gelatin and mix well to create a uniform powder blend that takes about 10 minutes.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with approximately 500 milligrams of the powder blend for a total yield of 100 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation, regulate hormones, and calm the nervous system. The curcumin in turmeric has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and mood swings associated with PMS. Additionally, turmeric's adaptogenic properties help balance hormones and reduce stress, leading to a sense of emotional stability and well-being during this time.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of PMS such as bloating, cramps, and breast tenderness.

Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that contribute to PMS symptoms like mood swings, anxiety, and depression by protecting cells from damage and reducing oxidative stress.

Prostaglandin-regulating properties: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds can help regulate prostaglandins, hormone-like substances involved in menstrual cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness, thereby alleviating PMS symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in alleviating PMS symptoms such as bloating and cramps.
Roots: Containing a variety of bioactive compounds, including turmeric oil, which has been traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders and relieve symptoms associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality berberis aristata roots and stems, ensuring freshness and absence of contamination.
Clean and chop the harvested plant material into small pieces, removing any debris or impurities.
Dry the chopped plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a drying machine, for 7 to 10 days, at 60°c.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking care not to generate heat.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powdered berberis aristata herb, dosing approximately 250-500mg per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain natural compounds that target specific symptoms. The anxiety-reducing properties of kava can alleviate feelings of stress and tension, while its anti-inflammatory effects may help ease bloating and breast tenderness. Additionally, kava's sedative properties can promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted during PMS. By addressing these physical and emotional symptoms, herbal kava capsules can provide relief from the discomforts associated with premenstrual syndrome.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Kava's anxiolytic effects may help reduce feelings of anxiety and stress commonly experienced during PMS, promoting relaxation and emotional balance.

Analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties: Kava's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate physical symptoms such as breast tenderness, bloating, and cramping associated with PMS.

Serotonin-enhancing effects: Kava has been shown to increase serotonin levels in the brain, which can help regulate mood swings, irritability, and other emotional symptoms often experienced during PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in kavalactones, which have been shown to reduce anxiety and stress symptoms associated with PMS.
Rhizomes: Contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as bloating and breast tenderness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality piper methysticum roots from a trusted supplier or grow your own in ideal conditions.
Dry the harvested roots in a clean, dry environment away from direct sunlight and moisture for 7-10 days.
Grind 200g of dried piper methysticum roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with 100g of organic vegetable glycerin and 50g of tapioca starch in a clean container, stirring well for 5 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with a precise amount of the herbal mixture, approximately 500mg per capsule, and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain flavonoids, which have a calming effect on the body. These flavonoids help to reduce anxiety and stress levels, common symptoms of PMS. Additionally, hawthorn's vasodilatory properties can help alleviate breast tenderness and swelling associated with hormonal fluctuations. By promoting relaxation and reducing physical discomfort, hawthorn capsules offer a natural and effective way to manage PMS symptoms, allowing women to feel more balanced and comfortable during this time.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Hawthorn capsules contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and epicatechin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with PMS symptoms.

Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds have a regulatory effect on hormonal balance, particularly estrogen and progesterone levels, which can help alleviate PMS symptoms such as breast tenderness, mood swings, and fatigue.
Oleic acid: Hawthorn capsules contain oleic acid, a monounsaturated fatty acid with anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce pain, cramping, and inflammation associated with PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate PMS symptoms such as bloating and mood swings.
Fruits: High in anthocyanins and other polyphenols, which have antioxidant effects that reduce inflammation and improve overall hormonal balance, alleviating symptoms of PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry sufficient quantity of crataegus monogyna leaves and stems in a warm place for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 200mg of powdered crataegus monogyna with 100mg of gelatin and 50mg of glycerin to form a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, ensuring each capsule contains precisely 250mg of the blend.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze them for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain menthol, a natural compound that soothes digestive issues and alleviates symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and nausea. The peppermint oil in the capsules also has a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that often accompany PMS. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help to ease headaches and fatigue, making it an effective natural remedy for managing the discomforts associated with premenstrual syndrome.
remedy_properties: Digestive Relief: Peppermint capsules contain menthol, which helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce cramps, bloating, and gas associated with PMS.

Antispasmodic Properties: The active compound, menthol, also has antispasmodic properties that help to relieve menstrual cramps, headaches, and mood swings by reducing uterine contractions and improving blood flow.

Anti-inflammatory Effects: Peppermint capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the body, which may contribute to PMS symptoms such as breast tenderness, bloating, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other bioactive compounds, peppermint roots help alleviate symptoms of PMS such as bloating and cramps.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes contain a high concentration of menthone, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease menstrual pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a trusted source at the peak of their potency.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator set at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours.
Grind the dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix one part peppermint powder with three parts silica gel and fill empty capsules with the mixture weighing approximately 500 milligrams each.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat premenstrual syndrome with herbal capsules is to combine a formula that includes chasteberry, also known as Vitex agnus-castus, which helps regulate hormonal imbalances. Additionally, valerian root and St. John's Wort can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, while ginger and peppermint can soothe digestive issues often associated with PMS.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to premenstrual syndrome that are treated with herbal capsules are menopausal symptoms, such as hot flashes and night sweats, perimenopausal symptoms like mood swings and anxiety, and hormonal imbalances like thyroiditis or adrenal fatigue. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like black cohosh, dong quai, and St. John's Wort have been traditionally used to alleviate these symptoms by regulating hormone levels, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation.
